summary: Wego Inquisitor — a robust, extensible discord.js bot for the Wego server (public repo): new members are muted and only receive the 'New Wegos' role after a 1-hour delay, plus slash commands like /give-rank with autocomplete suggestion boxes. Secrets stay in a git-ignored .env; only placeholders are committed.

sections.body: On guildMemberAdd the member is recorded as pending (persisted to data/pending-members.json) instead of being given a role. A polling scheduler grants the New Wegos role once the configured delay (default 60m) elapses, and catches anyone whose delay passed while the bot was offline. /setup denies send-message permissions to @everyone and allows them for New Wegos across all channels, so pending members stay muted until verified.

/give-rank @user <rank> grants a role; the rank option uses Discord autocomplete (arrow-key suggestion box) sourced from roles the bot can manage / the ASSIGNABLE_RANKS allowlist. /setup locks channels; /ping is a health-check example. interactionCreate routes both command execution and autocomplete.

Commands (src/commands) and events (src/events) are auto-loaded from their folders — adding a feature is dropping in one file. Config is centralised and validated in config.js; persistence sits behind a small swappable JsonStore interface; a leveled logger and deploy-commands script round it out.

milestones.t: Delayed New Wegos role assignment after 1 hour
Live-tested against a real Discord server
